nodes.name: Pollution
nodes.description: Ambient air-pollution exposure where the patient lives. Most people (~90%) are in low-exposure environments.

nodes.name: Smoker
nodes.description: Whether the patient smokes. Strong independent driver of cancer risk.

nodes.name: Cancer
nodes.description: Whether the patient has lung cancer. Background rate ~0.1% rises to ~5% for smokers in high-pollution environments. Only directly observable through downstream tests and symptoms.

nodes.name: X-ray
nodes.description: Chest X-ray result. 90% sensitive to cancer but has a 20% false-positive rate — the result must be read in the context of other evidence.

nodes.name: Dyspnea
nodes.description: Whether the patient reports shortness of breath. Reported by ~65% of cancer cases but also ~30% of non-cancer patients, so weakly diagnostic on its own.
